name: Cardiff Council
description: The unitary local authority for Cardiff, responsible for education, social services, housing and economic development across the city.

name: Cardiff University
description: Russell Group university and a major anchor institution for the city's higher education, research and widening-participation activity.

name: Cardiff and Vale College
description: The principal further education college serving Cardiff and the Vale of Glamorgan, with vocational training across construction, health, digital and creative sectors.

name: Cardiff City FC Foundation
description: Community arm of Cardiff City Football Club, delivering employability, education and inclusion programmes for young people across the city.

name: Cardiff Capital Region
description: The 10-local-authority Cardiff Capital Region city deal partnership, with a remit for regional skills, transport and economic investment across SE Wales.

name: 3SC (Cardiff Third Sector Council)
description: Specialist employability subcontractor working with third-sector partners across Wales to deliver inclusion-focused employment support.

name: Senedd Cymru / Welsh Parliament
description: The democratically elected parliament for Wales, responsible for devolved policy and budget oversight including education, skills and social mobility.

name: Welsh Government
description: The devolved government for Wales, with responsibility for the Young Person's Guarantee, the Youth Engagement and Progression Framework and the Socio-economic Duty.

name: Grange Pavilion
description: Community-led pavilion in Grangetown providing a job club, skills support, youth activities and a cultural programme rooted in the local neighbourhood.

name: Welsh Refugee Council
description: National charity supporting refugees and asylum seekers in Wales with advice, integration, ESOL, employability and rights-based casework.

name: Llamau
description: Wales' leading homelessness charity for young people and women, providing accommodation, mediation, education and trauma-informed support.

name: The Wallich
description: Pan-Wales homelessness charity providing rough sleeper support, hostels, supported housing and recovery services with a harm-reduction approach.

name: Bawso
description: Specialist provider supporting Black, Asian and minoritised individuals and families affected by domestic abuse, trafficking and FGM across Wales.

name: EYST Wales (Ethnic Youth Support Team)
description: Welsh charity supporting Black, Asian and minoritised young people and families with mentoring, advocacy, employment and integration work.

name: Women Connect First
description: Cardiff-based charity supporting Black, Asian and minoritised women through education, employability training, ESOL and wellbeing support.

name: Hijinx Theatre
description: Cardiff-based inclusive theatre company creating performance and training opportunities with learning-disabled and autistic actors.

name: Cwmpas
description: Co-operatives and social-enterprise development agency (formerly the Wales Co-op Centre); delivery partner for Digital Communities Wales.

name: Engage to Change
description: Cardiff University-led supported-employment programme for young people aged 16-25 with learning disabilities and autism, in partnership with DFN Project SEARCH.

name: Challenge Wales
description: Cardiff Bay-based youth development charity using sail-training voyages to build confidence, teamwork and resilience in young people aged 12-25.

name: One Million Mentors (Cardiff Capital Region)
description: Cardiff Capital Region branch of the One Million Mentors programme, matching young people with trained career mentors.

name: Admiral Group
description: FTSE 100 insurance group headquartered in Cardiff; major local employer with a substantial early-careers and community programme.

name: South Wales Police
description: Territorial police force covering Cardiff and the wider South Wales region, with neighbourhood policing and youth diversion teams.

name: Tarian (South Wales Regional Organised Crime Unit)
description: Regional organised-crime unit covering Dyfed-Powys, Gwent and South Wales police forces, with prevention work targeting vulnerable young people.

name: ReStart: Refugee Integration Project
description: Cardiff-based programme supporting newly-recognised refugees through housing, employment, English language and integration casework.

name: YMCA Cardiff
description: Cardiff branch of the YMCA, delivering youth work, housing for young people and family programmes across the city.

name: The King's Trust
description: UK-wide youth charity (formerly The Prince's Trust) supporting young people aged 11-30 into work, training and self-employment. Cardiff regional office.

name: Shaw Trust
description: UK-wide employability and skills charity delivering Welsh Government contracts including Communities for Work and supported employment. Cardiff regional base.

name: Platfform
description: Wales' mental-health and social-change charity, working with people facing distress, trauma and inequality across communities and individual support.

name: Adferiad
description: Welsh charity supporting people with co-occurring mental-health and substance-use needs, formed from the merger of CAIS, Hafal and Welsh Council on Alcohol and Other Drugs.

name: Windsor Fellowship (LP Wales/Cymru)
description: Charity running leadership and personal-development programmes for young people from minoritised and under-represented backgrounds. LP Wales/Cymru is the Welsh delivery arm.

name: The Brilliant Club (Wales)
description: Education charity placing doctoral researchers in schools to support disadvantaged pupils' progression to highly-selective universities. Wales hub.

name: Cardiff and Vale University Health Board
description: The NHS health board serving Cardiff and the Vale of Glamorgan, with public health, mental health and community health responsibilities relevant to social mobility outcomes.

name: Hodge
description: Welsh-headquartered financial services group at One Central Square specialising in commercial lending and later-life lending, with an established Cardiff apprenticeship intake.

name: Principality Building Society
description: Wales's largest building society, headquartered on Queen Street in Cardiff city centre, with branch and head-office roles and a financial services apprenticeship programme.

name: Legal & General (Cardiff)
description: Major financial services employer with a long-established Cardiff operations site at Brunel House, recruiting into customer operations and operations apprenticeships.

name: BBC Cymru Wales
description: BBC Wales national broadcaster based at Central Square in central Cardiff, with apprenticeship and trainee schemes across production, journalism and broadcast engineering.

name: Hugh James
description: Cardiff-headquartered law firm with a recognised solicitor apprenticeship pathway alongside conventional trainee routes.

name: Cardiff Bus
description: Council-owned bus operator running most local routes from depots at Sloper Road; a steady employer of bus drivers, engineers and apprentices.

name: Confused.com (RVU)
description: Price-comparison technology company based at The Friary in central Cardiff, with tech-apprentice and graduate engineering pipelines.

name: IQE plc
description: Cardiff-headquartered semiconductor wafer manufacturer at St Mellons, employing engineering apprentices and technicians in advanced manufacturing.

name: Dŵr Cymru Welsh Water
description: Not-for-profit water and wastewater utility for Wales with a major Cardiff operations base at Linea, Fortran Road, St Mellons; runs apprenticeships across engineering, customer ops and lab science.

name: Reach plc (Wales Online / WalesLive)
description: Wales newsroom of the UK's largest commercial news publisher, based at Six Park Street in central Cardiff, with journalism trainee schemes.
